---
title: "Clean Permitted Events"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("data.table")
}
library(data.table)
```

```{r}
# Paths
input_path <- "Datasets/NYC_Permitted_Event_Information_-_Historical_20260121.csv"
output_path <- "Datasets/Clean/NYC_Permitted_Events_Clean.csv"

# Date filter window aligned to complaints data
min_date <- as.IDate("2006-01-01")
max_date <- as.IDate("2024-12-31")

# Columns to keep (no Event ID, per request)
keep_cols <- c(
  "Event Name",
  "Start Date/Time",
  "End Date/Time",
  "Event Agency",
  "Event Type",
  "Event Borough",
  "Event Location",
  "Event Street Side",
  "Street Closure Type",
  "Community Board",
  "Police Precinct"
)

# Load
dt <- fread(input_path)

# Keep relevant columns if present
keep_present <- intersect(keep_cols, names(dt))
dt <- dt[, ..keep_present]

# Parse datetimes robustly
parse_dt <- function(x) {
  x <- trimws(as.character(x))
  x[x == ""] <- NA
  fmts <- c(
    "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M",
    "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S",
    "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M",
    "%m/%d/%Y",
    "%Y-%m-%d",
    "%Y %b %d %I:%M:%S %p",
    "%Y %B %d %I:%M:%S %p"
  )
  out <- as.POSIXct(rep(NA, length(x)), tz = "America/New_York")
  for (fmt in fmts) {
    idx <- is.na(out) & !is.na(x)
    if (!any(idx)) break
    parsed <- as.POSIXct(strptime(x[idx], fmt, tz = "America/New_York"))
    out[idx][!is.na(parsed)] <- parsed[!is.na(parsed)]
  }
  out
}

dt[, `Start Date/Time` := parse_dt(`Start Date/Time`)]
dt[, `End Date/Time` := parse_dt(`End Date/Time`)]

# If end is missing, set to start
dt[is.na(`End Date/Time`) & !is.na(`Start Date/Time`), `End Date/Time` := `Start Date/Time`]

# Filter to date range (keep rows that overlap the window)
dt <- dt[!is.na(`Start Date/Time`)]
dt[, start_date := as.IDate(`Start Date/Time`)]
dt[, end_date := as.IDate(`End Date/Time`)]
dt <- dt[start_date <= max_date & end_date >= min_date]
dt[, c("start_date", "end_date") := NULL]

# Drop columns that are entirely NA or empty strings
nonempty_cols <- names(dt)[
  vapply(
    dt,
    function(x) {
      if (is.character(x)) {
        any(!is.na(x) & trimws(x) != "")
      } else {
        any(!is.na(x))
      }
    },
    logical(1)
  )
]
dt <- dt[, ..nonempty_cols]

# Write cleaned dataset
fwrite(dt, output_path)
```
